items.title: Barstool's Portnoy plans to hold bitcoin down to zero after timing it wrong every time
items.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy said he will hold bitcoin all the way down to zero after buying near $100,000.

items.title: Tokenization's next use case is personalized portfolios, NYLIM executive says
items.description: Thomas Sy, head of multi-asset solutions at the $800-million asset manager, says blockchain can enable complex portfolio construction that's not yet possible in traditional finance.

items.title: Bitcoin jumps above $63,000, reversing end-June losses
items.description: The token hit its highest in over a month during thin July 4 trading, with XRP up 5% in 24 hours to lead gains among majors.

items.title: Bitcoin experts split over plan to freeze Satoshi's 1.1 million bitcoin as quantum threat grows
items.description: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao said Satoshi Nakamoto's bitcoin should be frozen before quantum computers can steal it. Not everyone agrees.

items.title: How ethical hackers with just a $3,000 server found a flaw that could've put $70 billion in crypto at risk
items.description: A critical flaw in the Aptos blockchain, which was patched, gave researchers a near-90% success rate at breaking a core security guarantee, with attack costs of just hundreds of dollars.

items.title: Why bitcoin's disconnect from record-high stocks won't last
items.description: Researchers at Schwab and Hashdex said AI has diverted capital from digital assets while bitcoin continues to follow a familiar post-halving recovery pattern.

items.title: Trump's crypto token buyers are down $3.8 billion, blockchain data shows
items.description: The TRUMP token is down 96% from its peak, and 85% of secondary market wallets for WLFI are underwater, reflecting a broader downturn in the sector.

items.title: Europe led on crypto regulation. Now implementation must match ambition
Binance outflows triple to $1.2B as ETH withdrawals hit 3-year high
items.description: Binance recorded $1.23 billion in weekly net outflows, up 207% from the previous week, as Ethereum withdrawals surged to a three-year high.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin shares top priorities for new 'Lean Ethereum' strawmap
items.description: Part of the Ethereum Foundation’s plan to make Ethereum more private and scalable is to introduce a new virtual machine, with leanISA and RISC-V among the top candidates.

items.title: Moonbeam to pivot from Polkadot to Base, unveils AI agent framework
items.description: Moonbeam didn’t provide a timeline for when it would launch the AI agent platform but told GLMR holders to bridge their tokens from the Polkadot parachain to Base before July 31.

items.title: Kraken lets traders use tokenized stocks as collateral for leveraged trades
items.description: Eligible users can now use select tokenized stocks and ETFs as collateral for futures and margin trading without selling their holdings.

items.title: Kalshi hits record June trading volume as World Cup fuels prediction markets
items.description: DefiLlama data shows Kalshi posted a record month for trading volume as the expanded FIFA World Cup boosted prediction market activity.

items.title: Bollinger Bands creator eyes Bitcoin bear-market end, 'W'-shaped reversal
items.description: Bitcoin price strength could "break" the entire downtrend if the reversal pattern completed, John Bollinger suggested in light of the BTC rebound.

items.title: Revolut to delist USDT in August, citing regulatory and risk concerns
items.description: Revolut notified some customers it will delist USDT after Aug. 31, with remaining holdings automatically converted into users’ base currency.

items.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
items.description: The SpaceX stock purchases come as Elon Musk’s company deepens its role in federal contracting and remains closely tied to President Donald Trump’s Washington.

items.title: The World Cup sends prediction market volumes soaring to record highs
items.description: Trading volumes on Kalshi and Polymarket both hit record highs in June. Rothera, a new prediction market platform, was also able to manage $2 billion in volume.

items.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
items.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

items.title: Iran begins six-day funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei nearly four months after his death
items.description: Iran plans a six-day funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with ceremonies across Iran and Iraq before burial in Mashhad.

items.title: 5 takeaways from President Donald Trump's interview with CNBC
items.description: The president defended his family's business and said he feels bad that the presidency creates a conflict of interest in any of his children's investments.

items.title: UK and France agree with Oman to ensure safety of its territorial waters
items.description: France said it has deployed mine countermeasures to the Middle East, including two mine-hunting ships.

items.title: Feds seek lower prison term for $100M New Jersey deli fraudster — but some reasons why are hidden
items.description: James Patten is the third person who will be sentenced in the stock manipulation scheme related to Hometown International, a company that owned just one deli.

items.title: From Macron to Modi, governments are rolling out the red carpet for AI giants
items.description: Macron and Modi are courting tech CEOs as France and India seek AI data center investment and cloud infrastructure.

items.description: Kyiv’s intensified drone campaign is spurring Russia’s worst fuel crisis in decades

items.title: How Bending Spoons built a $23bn tech empire from struggling brands
items.description: Nasdaq listing caps more than a decade of deals that transformed an Italian start-up into a global internet company

items.title: Returning to pre-Trumpian politics won’t save America
items.description: The opening of the Obama presidential library set off a wave of nostalgia for an era that is not coming back

items.title: Donald Trump defies storms and heatwave to deliver July 4 address
items.description: President eulogises forebears and eviscerates enemies at Lincoln Memorial on 250th anniversary of independence

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6fnsXPgaEjk! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Gamer trades in $1,000 of physical discs at GameStop, days after Sony announces end of disc era
items.description: While some collectors may plan to hoard their physical media, one Columbus gamer decided to liquidate his collection

items.title: ‘She wants him gone’: My friend took in a homeless man as a caretaker. After 10 years, how can she evict him?
items.description: “He lived rent-free, helping her with health-related issues and other tasks.”

items.title: Here’s what’s worth streaming in July 2026 on Netflix, Hulu, HBO Max and more
items.description: Why it’s a good month to save on streaming, despite the return of ‘Enola Holmes’ to Netflix and ‘Silo’ to Apple

items.title: Why a founder at age 50 is twice as likely to find success as one at age 30
items.description: Older workers are fighting ageism by starting their own businesses — and they’re outperforming younger entrepreneurs

items.title: Why June’s jobs and inflation data are bullish for bonds
items.description: The new jobs report is worse than many people realize.

items.title: Insurers shifted roof-replacement costs onto homeowners thanks to a new federal rule — just in time for hail and hurricane season
items.description: Those who find extensive damage to their roofs might be stuck between two options: File an insurance claim, which could trigger a premium increase, or cough up the cash for the necessary repairs or replacement.

items.title: Where can I invest my kid’s ‘Trump account’ money? The Treasury Department just answered that question.
items.description: The money in “Trump accounts” has to be invested in low-cost index funds — and now parents and investors are learning which funds they can actually use.

items.title: Jelly Roll’s ex-wife Bunnie Xo says he gave her their $6 million Tennessee estate in divorce
items.description: Country singer Jelly Roll’s ex-wife, Bunnie Xo, has revealed that she will take full ownership of the former couple’s Tennessee compound as part of their divorce settlement.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l0tWlr5gwIE! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

items.title: Ukraine striking Russian energy infrastructure at unprecedented rate
items.description: Kyiv’s intensified drone campaign is spurring Russia’s worst fuel crisis in decades

items.title: French push to exclude UK from EU defence spending backfires
items.description: Paris loses out on cheap loans due to strict eligibility criteria it had championed

items.title: Returning to pre-Trumpian politics won’t save America
items.description: The opening of the Obama presidential library set off a wave of nostalgia for an era that is not coming back

items.title: Octopus Energy set for showdown with workers over union recognition
items.description: Fast-growing energy supplier says employee share ownership means it’s not a ‘them and us’ company

items.title: High share prices are becoming a status symbol
items.description: Decline in stock splits suggests dividing share count no longer rules corporate orthodoxy

items.title: Uber stalls European food delivery push as it pursues Delivery Hero takeover
items.description: Ride-hailing company will no longer launch in five of its seven planned new markets this year

items.title: China cools on overseas publication of scientific research
items.description: Policymakers discuss reducing incentives for academics to submit papers to international journals over leak concerns

items.title: Burnham faces tricky electoral test as Starmer pushes through local government shake-up
items.description: Existing ministers argue major overhaul of city boundaries will be vital for his goal of devolving power

items.title: Taylor Swift’s Wedding Became a Marketing Moment for Brands Big and Small
items.description: Some businesses flocked to social media using A.I.-generated content, while others deployed timely digital ads designed to capitalize on the frenzy surrounding this major celebrity event.

items.title: I Have a Light Workload and I Simply Can’t Handle It
items.description: Plus: When a nurse practitioner in gynecology needs care, should she see one of her own colleagues?

items.title: The Revenge of the Philosophy Majors
items.description: A.I. labs are hiring contrarian, chin-stroking, finger-steepling sages. Who’s underemployed now?

items.title: Wikipedia Is Battling for the Soul of the Internet
items.description: The internet’s largest stockpile of free knowledge is under threat from MAGA, A.I. and foreign autocrats. A bibliophile ex-ambassador is here to help.

items.title: Nearly a Million Investors Lost a Total of $3.8 Billion on Trump Crypto Coin
items.description: A report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm details how those who bought the Trump memecoin have fared, with most retail investors having lost money while sophisticated traders did better.

items.title: Are the ‘MANGOS’ Stocks Already Turning Soft?
items.description: Wall Street loves an acronym. The latest one stands for Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia and three other companies at the center of the artificial intelligence boom.

items.title: The Rise of Racetrack Country Clubs: ‘Racing Is Golf on Steroids’
items.description: A motorsports club north of Miami, on former orange groves, is being built to cater to enthusiasts and well-heeled novices eager to hit the gas and pull some Gs.

items.title: David S. Doty, Judge Who Helped Shape the Modern N.F.L., Dies at 96
items.description: He presided over the 1993 agreement that led to free agency and the salary cap, and for years oversaw the league’s disputes with its players.
